reason: This passage (Genesis 12:1-9) contains Yahweh's covenant promise to Abram, emphasizing blessing, nation-building, and divine favor. The text focuses on divine blessings ('I will bless thee', 'thou shalt be a blessing', 'all families of the earth be blessed'). While it mentions cursing those who curse Abram ('curse him that curseth thee'), this refers to individual judgment, not a widespread public disaster. There is no mention of any destruction—man-made, natural, or supernatural—nor any disaster affecting a general population. The passage concludes with Abram's travels and altar-building, maintaining a positive, promissory tone throughout.
